questions.questionText: Schneider's first-rank symptoms of schizophrenia include:
questions.optionB: Auditory hallucinations (third person running commentary/voices discussing patient), thought withdrawal/insertion/broadcasting, delusions of control (made feelings/impulses/actions), somatic passivity
questions.explanation: Schneider's first-rank symptoms: highly suggestive of schizophrenia (but not pathognomonic). Auditory hallucinations: third-person running commentary, voices discussing patient, thought echo (gedankenlautwerden). Passivity phenomena: thought withdrawal (thoughts taken away), thought insertion (alien thoughts put in), thought broadcasting (thoughts known to others), delusions of control/made experiences. Positive symptoms: hallucinations, delusions, disorganized speech/behavior. Negative symptoms: flat affect, alogia, avolition, anhedonia, social withdrawal. Treatment: antipsychotics (typical: haloperidol — D2 blocker; atypical: risperidone, olanzapine, clozapine for refractory). Reference: Niraj Ahuja — Schizophrenia.
